summary: The first_proof_2 benchmark contains 10 research-level mathematics problems, of which 6 (prob-01, 02, 03, 04, 05, 07) have a resolved proof issue and zero open issues, indicating strong traction on those. Problems 06, 08, 09, and 10 have no recorded proof issues yet and represent the remaining frontier. The uniform 100-issue proof-status count across q1–q10 suggests an active, evenly distributed verification effort.
problems: No proof issues recorded for prob-06, prob-08, prob-09, and prob-10 — these problems lack any resolution activity and are the dataset's main gaps
All tracked problem issues are marked resolved with none open, which may mask undocumented work-in-progress or unverified resolutions
Proof status shows a uniform 100 issues per problem (q1–q10), an unusually flat distribution that may indicate auto-generated or boilerplate tracking rather than differentiated progress
Resolved proofs (prob-01 through prob-07) have not been cross-validated for correctness within the available metadata
highlights: Six of ten problems already have a resolved proof issue, a 60% resolution rate on tracked items

prob-01 (descriptive set theory), prob-02 (piecewise-linear Möbius), prob-03 (weighted Bernoulli sums), prob-04 (k-dilation inequalities), prob-05 (unique ergodicity), and prob-07 (quasi-reduced words) all show clean closure with zero open issues
Even 100-issue proof-status coverage across every problem indicates comprehensive tracking infrastructure is in place
The diversity of resolved domains demonstrates the team can make progress across multiple mathematical disciplines

suggested_todos.title: Initiate proof attempts and issue tracking for prob-06, prob-08, prob-09, and prob-10, which currently have no recorded activity
Independently verify the six resolved proofs (prob-01 through prob-07) to confirm correctness before marking the benchmark items final
Investigate the uniform 100-issue proof-status counts to determine whether they reflect genuine differentiated progress or placeholder data
Prioritize prob-06 and prob-08 as likely next solvable targets given the team's demonstrated breadth across related domains
Document resolution methods from the six closed problems to build reusable proof strategies for the remaining four
